title: Data Copy Facilities and Referential Integrity
breadcrumbs: Data Copy Facilities and Referential Integrity
text: Data Copy Facilities and Referential Integrity Referential integrity can be temporarily violated while loading large amounts of data in related entities. Uniface provides utilities that allow large-scale loading of data into Uniface; Uniface provides the Data Copy facility for loading data into databases. For more information, see Data Copy Facility . Referential integrity can be temporarily violated if one or more occurrences of a Many entity in a relationship is loaded before the related occurrence of the one entity has been loaded. The violation ceases as soon as the related occurrence of the one entity is also loaded. Uniface regularly commits at short intervals during large load operations (unless disabled with a connector option), because many DBMSs impose restrictions on the size of transactions. This can result in related occurrences being stored in separate transactions, which temporarily violates referential integrity. If a commit occurs in a DBMS, that is, referential integrity has been violated, the DBMS will rollback the transaction. For this reason, you need to temporarily disable the DBMS enforced referential integrity when performing a batch load operation. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
